name: pyesdl

metadata.name: pyESDL
metadata.summary: Python implementation of the Energy System Description Language (ESDL) for modelling energy systems

documentation:
# pyESDL

![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pyesdl)  ![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/pyESDL)  ![ReadTheDocs status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/pyesdl/badge/?version=latest)


pyESDL is a library for using ESDL in python programs. ESDL stands for Energy System Description
Language and is created to support developers creating, parsing, generating ESDL-files with the 
goal to create interoperable energy system transition tooling.

This package contains all the ESDL classes of the ESDL language and an `EnergySystemHandler`
that helps the developer to read and write ESDL-files.

More information about ESDL can be found at:
1. [ESDL gitbook documentation](https://energytransition.gitbook.io/esdl/) with a general introduction,
   example applications and some code samples.
2. [ESDL Model Reference documentation](https://energytransition.github.io/) that describes all the 
   classes and definitions in detail using a clickable UML diagram.

## Support functions:

PyESDL now comes with a lot of support functions for:

- handling profiles:
  - ProfileManager: reading profiles from a CSV, converting to and from esdl.DateTimeProfile or esdl.TimeSeriesProfile.
  - ExcelProfileManager: reading from and writing profiles to Excel files
  - InfluxDBProfileManager: reading from and writong profiles to InfluxDB (version 1.7)
- handling geometries: conversion to and from esdl geometries, WKT, WKB, GeoJSON, ...
- qunatity and units: unit conversion, converting a QuantityAndUnit to a string, building a QuantityAndUnit from a string

## Installing
PyESDL now comes with a lot of support functions for handling profiles, geometries and qunatity and units. This requires
additional dependencies. As not all users might need all functionalities, there are several ways to install pyESDL.

Use the following command to install the pyESDL python module including all dependencies from the PyPi registry:

`pip install pyESDL[all]`

To install the minimal version of pyESDL:

`pip install pyESDL`

To install the dependencies for additional profile functionalities (only required for support for Excel and InfluxDB):

`pip install pyESDL[profiles]`

To install the dependencies for handling geometries:

`pip install pyESDL[geometry]`

## Documentation

We will start soon with generating more documentation on [readthedocs](https://pyesdl.readthedocs.io/).

## Usage

### Example 1 - Creating an ESDL-file and saving it to disk
```python
from esdl.esdl_handler import EnergySystemHandler
from esdl import esdl
esh = EnergySystemHandler()
es = esh.create_empty_energy_system(name="ES1", es_description='Nice Energy System',
                                   inst_title='instance 1', area_title="Area 51")
print(es)
esh.save(filename="test.esdl")
```

### Example 2 - Loading an ESDL file and adding a WindTurbine to an area
```python
from esdl.esdl_handler import EnergySystemHandler
from esdl import esdl
from pprint import pprint

esh = EnergySystemHandler()
# load an ESDL file and use type hinting to help the IDE to do auto completion
es: esdl.EnergySystem = esh.load_file('test.esdl')
print(es)
# Create a WindTurbine
wind_turbine = esdl.WindTurbine(name='WindTurbine at coast', rotorDiameter=50.0, height=100.0,
                               type=esdl.WindTurbineTypeEnum.WIND_ON_COAST)

# print the wind turbines properties in ESDL as a dict
pprint(esh.attr_to_dict(wind_turbine))

# Get the area where this windturbine should be added
area51: esdl.Area = es.instance[0].area
# add the WindTurbine to the area
area51.asset.append(wind_turbine)
# save the file
esh.save()

# Give the WindTurbine a location on the map
location = esdl.Point(lat=52.6030475337285, lon=4.729614257812501)
wind_turbine.geometry = location

esh.save()
# Convert the Energy System to an XML string and print it
xml_string = esh.to_string()
print(xml_string)
```

### Converting ESDL units
Example:
```python
from esdl.units.conversion import convert_to_unit, ENERGY_IN_J, ENERGY_IN_MWh

converted = convert_to_unit(5, ENERGY_IN_MWh, ENERGY_IN_J)
18E9 == converted
>> True
```

## Changes

## Version 24.11.1
- Downgrade pyEcore to 0.13.2 again, due to error with rows in tables

## Version 24.11
- Update build system and dependencies
- Fix wrong import in `support_functions` on Python > 3.12
- Update pyEcore to 0.15.1

## Version 24.9
- Add support for ESDL release 24.09
- Upgrade shapely and geojson versions

## Version 24.2
- Fix bug when no tags are given for an InfluxDBProfile
- Fix issue with multi-valued attribute values (such as values in TableRow)
- Start with the ReadTheDocs documentation

### Version 24.1
- Add support for ESDL release 24.01 

### Version 23.12.1
- Fix bug in ProfileManager
- InfluxDBProfileManager.load_influxdb now returns an esdl.InfluxDBProfile
- InfluxDBProfileManager: improved handling of situations where no data is returned

### Version 23.12
- Bug fixes in InfluxDBProfileManager

### Version 23.11.2
- Add support for ESDL release 23.11 (with powerCurveTable for WindTurbine)

### Version 23.11.1
- Fix bug in determining the end_datetime of profiles

### Version 23.11
- Implement EDR client
- Added more predefined QaUs
- Support for tags in InfluxDBProfileManager

### Version 23.10.1
- Implement set_profile function
- Corrected some bugs (datetime_utils missing, support for fields with '-')

### Version 23.10
- Add support functions for handling profiles, geometries and qunatity and units.

### Version 23.03
- Add support for ESDL release 23.03 (added 2 PowerPlant types, referenceYear for CostInformation, added fullLoadHours and operationalHours attributes to Consumer and Transport classes)

### Version 22.11.1
- Add support for ESDL release 22.11 (with KPI-KPI and Sector-Sector relation, ElectricBoiler, PowerPlant types, ...)

### Version 22.11
- Add support for Energy Data Repository files (*.edd)
- Add supoprt for linking to external files (e.g. stored in the EDR) using `get_external_reference(url, object_id)`
- Add function to instantiate a class by its class name (`instantiate_esdltype(className)`) 

### Version 22.10.0
- Add support for ESDL release 22.10 (with Port-Constraint relation, support for modelling material flows, added powerFactor attribute for electricity related assets, DataSourceList)

### Version 22.7.0
- Add support for ESDL release 22.07 (with ConnectableAsset, ExposedPortsAsset, PumpedHydroPower and CAES assets, restructured some LabelJump information, asset Constraints)

### Version 21.12.0
- Add support for ESDL release 21.12 (with quantity and unit information for InputOutputRelation, renaming of some distribution information classes of AggregatedBuildings)

### Version 21.11.0
- Add support for ESDL release 21.11 (with HybridHeatpump, developmentCosts, Commodity emission attribute)

 ### Version 21.10.0
- Add support for ESDL release 21.10 (with storage volumes)

### Version 21.9.1
- Add support for ESDL release 21.9 (with pipe and cable relations)

### Version 21.9.0
- Fix pyecore 0.12.1 dependency issue

### Version 21.7.1
- Add support for ESDL release 21.7 (with BufferDistance, ATES)

#### Version 21.6.2
- Fix issue with version definition, making the EnergySystemHandler unusable.
- Add get_all_instances_of_type() to EnergySystemHandler to retrieve all the instances of a certain type in an EnergySystem.
  
  E.g. ```esh.get_all_instances_of_type(esdl.GenericProfile)``` will give you all the profiles defined in the EnergySystem.

#### Version 21.6.1
- Add support for InputOutputRelation 


## Releasing
The release process is as follows:
1. Do a `git tag` with the new version number
2. Do `python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel` to create the tarball and wheel in the `dist` folder
3. upload to PyPi: `python3 -m twine upload --repository pyESDL dist/*`

modules.name: esdl.resources.json
documentation:
The json module introduces JSON resource and JSON parsing.
This is the json module from pyEcore 0.15

methods.name: esdl.resources.json.JsonResource.to_dict
documentation: @singledispatchmethod
def to_dict(self, obj, is_ref=False):
return obj
@to_dict.register(type)
def _(self, obj, is_ref=False):
if not issubclass(obj, EObject):
return obj
if is_ref:
fun = self._to_ref_from_obj
return fun(obj.eClass, self.options, self.use_uuid, self)
@to_dict.register(EEnumLiteral)
def _(self, obj, is_ref=False):
return obj.name
@to_dict.register(EObject)
def _(self, obj, is_ref=False):
if is_ref:
fun = self._to_ref_from_obj
else:
cls = obj.eClass.python_class
mapper = next((self.mappers[k] for k in self.mappers
if issubclass(cls, k)), self.default_mapper)
fun = mapper.to_dict_from_obj
return fun(obj, self.options, self.use_uuid, self)
@to_dict.register(ECollection)
def _(self, obj, is_ref=False):
fun = self._to_ref_from_obj if is_ref else self.to_dict
result = []
for x in obj:
write_object = fun(x)
if write_object is NO_OBJECT:
continue
result.append(write_object)
return result

variables.name: esdl.resources.xmlresource.logger
documentation:
Extension of pyecore's XMIResource to support the XMLResource in EMF.
It basically removes the xmi:version stuff from the serialization.
It also adds parse_information that can be inspected for errors when reading an ESDL file.

modules.name: esdl.resources.xmi
documentation:
Temporary replace xmi.py from pyEcore with our own as multi-valued attributed values are not correctly read from the
xmi file. In pyEcore 0.13 + 0.14 they used () tuple notation instead of [] for multi-valued attribute values causing it to return a
generator for those values instead of a list of values.

The xmi module introduces XMI resource and XMI parsing.

functions.name: esdl.units.conversion.convert_to_unit
documentation:
Converts a value from the source_unit into a target_unit

example: converted_value: float = convert_to_unit(10, ENERGY_IN_J, ENERGY_IN_MWh)

```
param value

the value that needs to be converted

param source_unit

the QuantityAndUnit of the source (can also be a reference)

param target_unit

the QuantityAndUnit of the target (can also be a reference, they are resolved to a Type)

return

the converted value
```

functions.name: esdl.units.conversion.convert_unit
documentation:
Does some basic unit conversion, only Joule to Wh, Wh to Joule and °C to Kelvin and vice versa.
Can only convert units when physical quantities are the same (e.g. Energy, Temperature)

functions.name: esdl.units.conversion.get_attribute_unit
documentation:
Retrieves the unit defined in the ESDL ecore schema for a specific attribute

example: get_attribute_unit("PowerPlant", "power"), or

: get_attribute_unit(esdl.PowerPlant, "power"), or
  get_attribute_unit(myPowerPlant, "power")

```
param esdl_class

the ESDL class (as string, ESDL class or ESDL Object)

param attribute

string describing the attribute of the esdl_class

return

a string with the unit (e.g. W, m, years)
```

methods.name: esdl.esdl_handler.EnergySystemHandler.get_external_reference
documentation:
Support function to create a reference to an external ESDL/EDD file.

```
param url

web address e.g. a URL from the EDR, https://drive.esdl.hesi.energy/store/resource/edr/Public/Key figures/Emissiefactoren energiedragers 2017.edd

param object_id

Optional, the id of the object in the file to refer to, if not specified, it will return the root of the file.

return

EObject returning the root of the document, or the object that is specified by the id found in that file
```

Be aware that when assigning an external reference to a containment relation, the content from the external reference
will be moved (not copied) to the resource that it is assigned to. If you want the reference to be external,
only use this for references (no diamond), not containment relations (a diamond in the ESDL UML diagram)

methods.name: esdl.esdl_handler.EnergySystemHandler.get_by_id
documentation: Using this function you can query for objects by ID
When loading an ESDL-file, all objects that have an ID defined are stored in resource.uuid_dict automatically
Note: If you add things later to the resource, it won't be added automatically to this dictionary though.
Use get_by_id_slow() for that

methods.name: esdl.esdl_handler.EnergySystemHandler.update_uuid_dict

This might be necessary when e.g. deepcopying an object in an resource that has references to other parts of that
resource that are not being deepcopied.
\:param es: energy system that needs a uuid_dict update, if None, the current self.energy_system is used

Creates a new empty Energy System (EnergySystem + Instance + Area) and adds it to the resource set
\:param name:
\:param es_description: Optional
\:param inst_title: optional, default "Instance0"
\:param area_title:
\:return: the created empty Energy System

methods.name: esdl.esdl_handler.EnergySystemHandler.__getstate__
documentation: Support for Pickling when serializing the energy system in a session
The pyEcore classes by default do not allow for simple serialization for Session management in Flask.
Internally Flask Sessions use Pickle to serialize a data structure by means of its __dict__. This does not work.
Furthermore, ESDL can contain cyclic relations. Therefore we serialize to XMI and back if necessary.
This is not efficient with large ESDLs

methods.name: esdl.esdl_handler.EnergySystemHandler.version
documentation: Returns the version of pyESDL

methods.name: esdl.esdl_handler.StringURI.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new StringURI to be used for a resource. By setting text it can convert that XML text to a
python class model, when doing a resource.load(uri). If text is empty and the associated resource is not,
resource.save(uri) will convert the resource to XML. Use uri.getvalue() to return the string representation of
this resource.
\:param uri: the file name of this ESDL, should be unique in this resource set.
\:param text: XML string to convert to a resource (pyEcore classes)

documentation:

"EnergyAsset", "Sectors", "Carriers" or "InfluxDBProfile". Each element in the returned list is of type EDRInfo
and has id, title, description and esdl_type fields. The ESDL type of the elements in the list can be subtypes
of the input esdl_type, e.g. you ask for objects of type "EnergyAsset" and get back objects of type

modules.name: esdl.esdl
documentation: Definition of meta model 'esdl'.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergySystem
documentation: This is the main class to describe an EnergySystem in ESDL. Each energy system description should start with this class. More information about ESDL and the Energy System can be found in the gitbook at https://energytransition.gitbook.io/esdl/

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Area
documentation: The Area class represents a physical geographic area or a more abstract logical area. In both cases it is the 'asset container', in a sense that all assets within the area are contained by the Area instance.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Port
documentation: Ports allow connections between EnergyAssets. Ports can be connected to one or more other ports. There are two types of ports: InPort and OutPort, which defines the primary direction of positive energy flow. InPorts can only be connected to OutPorts and vice versa.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EconomicProperties
documentation: (experimental) Can be used to define the economic properties of an area

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SocialProperties
documentation: (experimental) Can be used to define the social properties of an area

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Item
documentation: Class describing an abstract thing in an energy system. It is the parent of many other classes in ESDL, such as Assets, Services and Potentials. Parties can own Items

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Instance
documentation: Instances are used to represent different representations of the same EnergySystem. Most of the times only one Instance will be used. The primary use case for having more than one Instance is when you have different aggregations of the same EnergySystem in the same model (e.g. the same region on house level and aggregated on neighbourhood level). Another option would be to create different instances for different years (to describe the progress of the energy transition).

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Carriers
documentation: Collection of carriers as part of the Energy System Information. Both energy carriers and commodities.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergySystemInformation
documentation: Describes reusable information of the energy system, that other classes can refer to in this energy system, such as carriers, profiles, data sources, quantity and units.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericProfile
documentation: All profiles should describe these fields: a name and a ProfileType. There are two different profile types: static, with static values stored in the ESDL model itself. And External, which allows you to refer to an externally defined profile (e.g. in an Energy Information System or a timeseries database)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ProfileElement
documentation:
ProfileElement describes a single profile element describing a range and a value which is valid for this range. From-field is inclusive, To-field is exclusive, allowing you to describe ranges such as 1-1-2017T00:00:00.000 to 1-1-2018T00:00:00.000 instead of 31-12-2017T23:59:59:999. The to-field may be ommitted, meaning this value is valid for all time after the specified to-datetime.
Examples: The heat demand of a municipality in 2013 is 20 PJ. The range you define is then from 1-1-2013T to 1-1-2014T and the value 20 and ProfileType ENERGY_IN_PJ

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericDistribution
documentation: Abstract class to represent different types of distributions

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Percentile
documentation: Defines the percentile of a percentile distribution

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CostInformation
documentation: Describes the costs to acquire, install and maintain a certain asset.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.StringItem
documentation: Defines a label and a percentage, used in StringLabelDistribution

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyLabelBin
documentation: Defines a bin for an energy label with a percentage, used in EnergyLabelDistribution

classes.name: esdl.esdl.FromToItem
documentation: Defines a range and a percentage, used in the FromToDistribution class

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PItemStat
documentation: (experimental) Used to define statistical information

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractVariance
documentation: (experimental) Used to define statistical information

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Party
documentation: Defines a stakeholder in the energy system, to represent ownership

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Geometry
documentation: Abstract class to define the shape/location of an asset or area. Parent class of e.g. Point, Line and Polygon

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Carrier
documentation: Abstract class to define the carrier of energy, e.g. a energy carrier or a commodity (such as electricity)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Duration
documentation: Defines the duration of a profile query

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Profiles
documentation: Container for profiles in the Energy System Information where other profiles can refer to

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Parties
documentation: Container for parties that have a role in the energy system

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DataSources
documentation: Collection of datasources used in the energy system

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SubPolygon
documentation: Part of a Polygon used to describe the internal or external boundary

classes.name: esdl.esdl.MobilityFuelInformation
documentation: Collection of information about vehicles, fuels and efficiency

classes.name: esdl.esdl.VehicleFuelEfficiency
documentation: Information about vehicles, fuels and efficiency, used in MobilityFuelInformation

classes.name: esdl.esdl.MobilityProperties
documentation: (experimental) Can be used to define the mobility properties of an area

classes.name: esdl.esdl.NumberOfVehicles
documentation: (experimental) Provides the ability to define the number of vehicles of an area

classes.name: esdl.esdl.VehicleCount
documentation: (experimental) Defines the number of vehicles per vehicle type

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Services
documentation: Defines a collection of logical services used in the energy system, e.g. Demand-Response, Aggregator services, Energy markets and control strategies.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractDataSource
documentation: Abstract class to describe data sources or references to data sources

classes.name: esdl.esdl.KPIs
documentation: Collection of key performance indicators of areas or assets

classes.name: esdl.esdl.KPI
documentation: Defines a key performance indicator (KPI)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.QuantityAndUnits
documentation: Collection of QuantityAndUnitTypes defined in the EnergySystemInformation section

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractQuantityAndUnit
documentation: Abstract class to describe QuantityAndUnitTypes or references to these

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Parameters
documentation: Used to describe properties of an EnergyMarket

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Sectors
documentation: Collection of sectors. Both Party and Item can link to a sector

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Sector
documentation: Defines a sector. Can be used for the Standaard Bedrijfsindeling (SBI) of the CBS in the Netherlands

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractInstanceDate
documentation: Abstract class to define the date or period of the validity of the data that is used in this instance

classes.name: esdl.esdl.WeekSchedule
documentation: Specifies a week schedule for building usage

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DaySchedule
documentation: Specifies a day schedule as part of a week schedule. A day schedule is a collection of events with a timestamp

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Event
documentation: Event with a timestamp

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractBuildingUsage
documentation: Abstract class to support references to building usages

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingUsageInformation
documentation: Part of Energy System Information that specifies generic building usage information that can be referenced from multiple individual buildings

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingTypeBin
documentation: Defines a bin for a building type with a percentage, used in BuildingTypeDistribution

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ResidentialBuildingTypeBin
documentation: Defines a bin for a residential building type with a percentage, used in ResidentialBuildingTypeDistribution

classes.name: esdl.esdl.OwnershipRentalTypeBin
documentation: Defines a bin for a ownership/rental type with a percentage, used in OwnershipRentalTypeDistribution

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CompoundMatterComponent
documentation: One of the components of a CompoundMatter instance

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Templates
documentation: Collection of templates, e.g. asset templates.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Address
documentation: The address of a building or building unit.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingInformation
documentation: Super class of all different kinds of extra information that can be specified for a building

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Table
documentation: Table class that represents data in a table structure. Current examples are the pump curve table and a table describing the flowCoefficient of a checkvalve (relation between pressure drop and flow rate)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Notes
documentation: Collection of notes that can be added to the map, like postits (with comments in HTML)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Note
documentation: An individual note that can have a location on the map, to document certain decisions

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractBehaviour
documentation: Abstract class for specification of the (dynamic) behaviour of an asset

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PortRelation
documentation: Specifies the relation between a port and the main port using a specific ratio.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BufferDistance
documentation: Buffer distance around an asset that relates to risks, environment, noise, CO2, ...

classes.name: esdl.esdl.InPort
documentation: Represents a port with a positive energy direction into the asset, e.g. for a Consumer. See Port for more details

classes.name: esdl.esdl.OutPort
documentation: Represents a port with a positive energy direction out of the asset, e.g. for a Producer. See Port for more details

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Asset
documentation: Assets are all physical thing in the EnergySystem. Assets can have a location, a geometry, commissioning and decommissioning dates, cost information (investment, installation and operation and maintenance costs).

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Point
documentation: Describes a point geometry, which can be used for giving assets a location on a map

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Polygon
documentation: Describes a polygon geometry, which can be used for defining the shape of an area or building

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Measures
documentation: Collection of measures that can be applied to an energy system

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Service
documentation: Abstract class to represent logical entities in the energy system, e.g. demand response services, energy markets, etc.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Potential
documentation: Abstract class that represents energy potentials in an area, like wind potential, geothermal potential, residual heat source potential, etc.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyCarrier
documentation: Defines a carrier of energy with its emission and energy content properties

classes.name: esdl.esdl.StaticProfile
documentation: Stores the profile in the ESDL model itself, in contrast with an external profile, which refers to an external source for a profile

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ExternalProfile
documentation:
ExternalProfile allows to refer to an externally defined profile. Common uses are a profile defined in a (timeseries) database such as InfluxDB.
It allows you to specify a multiplier to scale the supplied external profile by a certain factor (e.g. when using NEDU profiles). Default the multiplier is '1'.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PercentileDistribution
documentation: Defines a distribution in terms of percentiles

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SpecificLabelDistribution
documentation: Abstract class to define a distribution with labels

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SymmetricVariance
documentation: (experimental) Used to define statistical information

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AssymmetricVariance
documentation: (experimental) Used to define statistical information

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DoubleAssymmetricVariance
documentation: (experimental) Used to define statistical information

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Line
documentation: Describes a line geometry based on a list of points, which can be used to define the shape of pipes and cables

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Commodity
documentation: Abstract class for commodities

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DataSource
documentation: A DataSource describes the source of the piece of information used in the energy system. E.g. a profile from NEDU or typical parameters of an Asset

classes.name: esdl.esdl.MultiPolygon
documentation: Collection of Polygons

classes.name: esdl.esdl.QuantityAndUnitType
documentation: Defines the quantity and its unit for a specific parameter. Used in e.g. profiles and KPIs. For example Energy in Joules or CO2 emission in kton.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DataSourceReference
documentation: Defines a reference to a datasource, defined in the collection of DataSources (as part of the EnergySystemInformation)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.QuantityAndUnitReference
documentation: Defines a reference to a QuantityAndUnitType defined in the collection of QuantityAndUnits (as part of the EnergySystemInformation)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.StringParameter
documentation: Defines a parameter of type String

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DoubleParameter
documentation: Defines a parameter of type Double

classes.name: esdl.esdl.IntegerParameter
documentation: Defines a parameter of type Integer

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BooleanParameter
documentation: Defines a parameter of type Boolean

classes.name: esdl.esdl.MultiLine
documentation: Defines a collection of lines

classes.name: esdl.esdl.InstanceDate
documentation: Describes the date of the validity of the data that is used in this instance

classes.name: esdl.esdl.InstancePeriod
documentation: Describes the period of the validity of the data that is used in this instance

classes.name: esdl.esdl.WKT
documentation: Well-Known Text (see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Well-known_text)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.WKB
documentation: Well-Known Binary (See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Well-known_text#Well-known_binary)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingUsage
documentation: Collection of information about the usage of a building, such as temperature set points and opening hours.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingUsageReference
documentation: Specifies a reference to building usage (such as opening hours)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DoubleKPI
documentation: Specifies a KPI value as a double

classes.name: esdl.esdl.StringKPI
documentation: Specifies a KPI value as a string

classes.name: esdl.esdl.IntKPI
documentation: Specifies a KPI value as an integer

classes.name: esdl.esdl.FromToIntItem
documentation: Specifies a percentage range as an integer value, as part of a distribution, e.g. for defining a period of years (1945-1960) in Aggregated Buildings

classes.name: esdl.esdl.FromToDoubleItem
documentation: Specifies a percentage range as an double value, as part of a distribution, e.g. for defining energy usage (2.5-5.0 GJ of hot tap water) in Aggregated Buildings

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Restriction
documentation: Allows to specify restrictions to measures

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AssetTemplate
documentation: Template for an asset. Can be used to specify a generic asset type where specific instances can refer to and inherit properties of.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ResidentialBuildingInformation
documentation: Class that contains extra information that can be specified for a residential building

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractMatter
documentation: Abstract class for describing Matters, can be instantiated as a subclass of Matter or as a MatterReference.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.InputOutputRelation
documentation: Describes the relation between one of the ports of an asset (the mainPort) and all other ports using a specific ratio. Can be used for conversion assets to specify how much of the carrier on the InPorts is required to produce an x amount of the carrier on the OutPort.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractTransferFunction
documentation: Abstract class for a TransferFunction for the specification of the behaviour of an asset

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Constraint
documentation: Allows to specify constraints for asset attributes.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.InstanceYear
documentation: Describes the year of the validity of the data that is used in this instance

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Insulation
documentation: Describes insulation that can be added to a building. The relation with the heat consumption is not defined and requires manual modelling

classes.name: esdl.esdl.LegalArea
documentation: Used to define an area in which its purpose is defined by legal authorities, such as restricted areas. E.g. in areas where water is extracted, it is not allowed to plan new UTES.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyService
documentation: Abstract class to represent logical entities in the energy system, e.g. demand response services, energy markets, etc.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.WindPotential
documentation: Defines the potential for wind energy. This class can be used instead of 'SearchAreaWind' in case there is more information available.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DateTimeProfile
documentation: Describes a profile using one or more Profile elements. Each element defines a from- and a to-datetime and a value which is valid for this range. The to-field may be ommitted, meaning this value is valid for all time after the to-date.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SingleValue
documentation:
A profile used to define a single value. This should be used when no information is present about the time. E.g. the price of a PV panel as currently known
When a model queries for a value from a certain date (and to a certain date), that information will be ignored and it will always return this value.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.StringLabelDistribution
documentation: Defines a distribution in terms of self-defined labels

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyLabelDistribution
documentation: Defines a distribution in terms of energy labels

classes.name: esdl.esdl.FromToDistribution
documentation: Defines a distribution in terms of 'from' and 'to'

classes.name: esdl.esdl.URIProfile
documentation: Describes a reference to a profile in an information system using a URI (e.g. a URI to a profile in Energy Information System (EIS))

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DatabaseProfile
documentation: Describes the fields of a generic database-based profile

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GasCommodity
documentation: Defines a gas commodity. This class can be used as an abstract way of modelling gas commodity and can be used in conjunction with electricity commodity and heat commodity. If more detailed modelling is necessary, use energy carriers.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HeatCommodity
documentation: Defines a heat commodity

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ElectricityCommodity
documentation: Defines an electricity commodity

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Range
documentation: Defines a range between two values. Optionally a mid value can be specified as for example a mean value or most plausible value.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SolarPotential
documentation: Defines the potential for solar energy. This class can be used instead of 'SearchAreaSolar' in case there is more information available.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ProfileReference
documentation: Used to refer to profiles defined in the Energy System Information section

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ResidualHeatSourcePotential
documentation: Defines the residual heat potential in a specific area.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyCommodity
documentation: Generic energy commodity, to be used in (national) energy balances (when the type of energy is not important)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Measure
documentation: A single measure or a combination of measures with collective cost information that can be applied to an energy system. An example of a measure-combination would be a combination of insulation and a heat pump.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractGTPotential
documentation: Abstract class to describe geothermal potential

classes.name: esdl.esdl.UTESPotential
documentation: Defines the potential for underground thermal energy storage (UTES). E.g. ATES or BTES potential

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BiomassPotential
documentation: Defines the biomass potential in a specific area.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Glazing
documentation: Allows to specify the glass of a building, e.g. for calculating heat loss

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SearchAreaWind
documentation: Specifies search areas for wind turbines. Search areas are a kind of 'legal' areas that have been appointed by the (local) government as possible areas for wind installations. Further research should give insight in the real potential (in terms of energy).

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SearchAreaSolar
documentation: Specifies search areas for solar installations. Search areas are a kind of 'legal' areas that have been appointed by the (local) government as possible areas for solar installations. Further research should give insight in the real potential (in terms of energy).

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingTypeDistribution
documentation: Specifies the way the building type is distributed in this area (e.g. Utility, Residential), specifing the percentage of buildings per type.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ResidentialBuildingTypeDistribution
documentation: Specifies the way the residential building type is distributed in this area (e.g. Vrijstaande Woning, Hoekwoning, Flatwoning), specifing the percentage of buildings per residential type.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.OwnershipRentalTypeDistribution
documentation: Specifies the way the housing type is distributed in this area (e.g. Owner occupied, Housing Association, Private Rental), specifing the percentage of buildings per housing type.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Matter
documentation:
Abstract class for describing matters. There are three subclasses:

- Material: for the materials of which Assets are made, but also for raw materials (e.g. water as an input for an electrolyzer)
- Fuels: for decomposing EnergyCarriers
- CompoundMatter for creating a mixture or a collection of Materials or Fuels

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingTypeRestriction
documentation: Defines a restriction on the type of building (the purpose for which the building is used).

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AreaTypeRestriction
documentation: Defines a restriction on the type of area (road, railway, built, water, ...)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.TemplatedAsset
documentation: An instantiated asset that is referring to an asset template and the specific asset. The asset template contains generic information, the specific asset contains specific information about this instance (e.g. geometry).

classes.name: esdl.esdl.MinimumLabelRestriction
documentation: Defines a restriction on the minimum label required. Allows to specify for example to only apply a heatpump in a house with energy label B or better.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.InitialValue
documentation: Can be used to explicitely set an initial value of a certain parameter. Used as input for simulation models that calculate this parameter over time, but need a value to initialize the model.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.MatterReference
documentation: can be used to refer to a Matter from the collection of Matters (part of EnergySystemInformation)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericTransferFunction
documentation: Generic transfer function with numerator and denominator

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DelayTransferFunction
documentation: Delay transfer function with a time constant

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CombinedTransferFunction
documentation: Combination of multiple transfer functions by addition of multiplication of individual components

classes.name: esdl.esdl.TimeSeriesProfile
documentation: Describes a profile of which the period between the values is constant. The series of values is indexed in time order as a sequence taken at successive equally spaced points in time. It starts at the startDateTime and every next value has intervalBetweenValues seconds between them.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ResidentialBuildingTypeRestriction
documentation: Defines a restriction on the residential type of the building (terraced, free standing, appartment, ...).

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingYearRestriction
documentation: Defines a restriction on the building year range

classes.name: esdl.esdl.RangedConstraint
documentation: Allow to specify a certain constraint as a range (with min and max values)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ConnectableAsset
documentation: Abstract class to group sub classes that have exposed ports or ports of its own

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyAsset
documentation: An abstract class that describes a connectable Asset using ports. EnergyAssets main subclasses contain the 5 capability type: Producer, Consumer, Storage, Conversion and Transport

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GeothermalPotential
documentation: Defines the geothermal potential in a specific area. This type focusses on temperature and depth of the well. See GeothermalEnergyPotental for class focussing on Energy

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DemandResponseService
documentation: Indicates a service supporting demand response in the energy system

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AggregatorService
documentation: Indicates a aggregator service exploiting flexibility in the energy system

classes.name: esdl.esdl.InfluxDBProfile
documentation: Describes a profile based on a measurement and field as part of an InfluxDB timeseries query

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ControlStrategy
documentation: Defines a control strategy for a specific asset

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyMarket
documentation: Defines an EnergyMarket of the energy system. A market is defined by specifying the assets that participate in this market.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GeothermalEnergyPotential
documentation: Defines the geothermal potential in a specific area. This type focusses on energy and depth of the well. See GeothermalPotental for class focussing on temperature

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CompoundMatter
documentation:
Composition of different Matters, either mixed (mix of gasses or liquids) or layered.

Examples of layered Matters
- a construction of a wall, roof, or floor with isolation
- double or triple glazing consisting of multiple layers
- a heatnetwork pipe or electrical cable consisting of multiple layers

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Fuel
documentation: a Fuel like wood, oil, gas, and so on.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Material
documentation: a Material like copper, aluminum, wood, stone, concrete, water, styrofoam, plaster

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ExposedPortsAsset
documentation: Abstract class with a reference to exposed ports. It's the super class of CompoundAsset and all Buildings

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Producer
documentation: An abstract class that describes EnergyAssets that can produce energy. It is one of the 5 capabilities in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Consumer
documentation: An abstract class that describes EnergyAssets that can consume energy. It is one of the 5 capabilities in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Storage
documentation: An abstract class that describes EnergyAssets that can store energy. It is one of the 5 capabilities in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Conversion
documentation: An abstract class that describes EnergyAssets that can convert one energy carrier into another. It is one of the 5 capabilities in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Transport
documentation: An abstract class that describes EnergyAssets that can transport energy. It is one of the 5 capabilities in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractBuilding
documentation: Describes the shared properties of building, building unit and aggregated building

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DrivenByDemand
documentation: Control strategy specifying that an asset is driven by the demand of one of the output ports

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DrivenBySupply
documentation: Control strategy specifying that an asset is driven by the supply of one of the input ports (used in ESDL-based simulation tools)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.DrivenByProfile
documentation: Control strategy specifying that an asset is driven by a profile specified in one of the ports (used in ESDL-based simulation tools)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.StorageStrategy
documentation: Control strategy specifying that a storage asset is driven by two profiles specifying the marginal cost to define its charging and discharging behavior (used in ESDL-based simulation tools)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CurtailmentStrategy
documentation: Control strategy that specifies a max power at which the production is curtailed

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PIDController
documentation: Control strategy specifying that an asset is driven by a PID controller (used in ESDL-based simulation tools)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CompoundAsset
documentation: Asset that can contain multiple other assets that belong together. Can for example be used for modelling a certain storage technology, that consists of individual processes for charging and discharging, next to the storage capability itself

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Battery
documentation: A battery can store electrical energy. This is a Storage capability

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AggregatedConsumer
documentation: Represents an aggregation of multiple consumers as one aggregated consumer. It allows you to reference the consumers it is aggregated of by using the aggregationOf reference. Can be used to aggregate a heterogeneous collection of consumers (e.g. of different types)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AggregatedProducer
documentation: Represents an aggregation of multiple producers as one aggregated producer. It allows you to reference the producers it is aggregated of by using the aggregationOf reference. Can be used to aggregate a heterogeneous collection of producers (e.g. of different types)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericConsumer
documentation: Generic consumer class that can be used in cases that the actual asset type is not important or not supported yet in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericProducer
documentation: Generic producer class that can be used in cases that the actual asset type is not important or not supported yet in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericStorage
documentation: Generic storage class that can be used in cases that the actual asset type is not important or not supported yet in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericTransport
documentation: Generic transport class that can be used in cases that the actual asset type is not important or not supported yet in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AggregatedTransport
documentation: Represents an aggregation of multiple transport assets as one aggregated transport asset. It allows you to reference the transport asset it is aggregated of by using the aggregationOf reference. Can be used to aggregate a heterogeneous collection of transport assets (e.g. of different types)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AggregatedStorage
documentation: Represents an aggregation of multiple storage assets as one aggregated storage asset. It allows you to reference the storage asset it is aggregated of by using the aggregationOf reference. Can be used to aggregate a heterogeneous collection of storage assets (e.g. of different types)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericBuilding
documentation: Represents a physical building

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HeatStorage
documentation: Generic heat storage asset with min and max temperatures

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Import
documentation: Represents a source that delivers imported energy into the current energy system. Used to model the rest of the energy system that is out of the current scope

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Export
documentation: Represents a consumer that consumes exported energy from the current energy system. Used to model the rest of the energy system that is out of the current scope

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HeatingDemand
documentation: Describes the heating demand of e.g. a household, area, etc.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ElectricityDemand
documentation: Describes the electricity demand of e.g. a household, area, etc.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GasDemand
documentation: Describes the gas demand of e.g. a household, area, etc. This can be used for all types of gasses (e.g. CO2, Natural Gas, Hydrogen, etc.)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EVChargingStation
documentation: Represents a charging station for electrical vehicles. Both single private-owned car chargers and public charging spaces can be modelled by this class

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AggregatedBuilding
documentation: Represents more than one building aggregated into one entity. It supports different types of aggregation, such as building type, energy label etc.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Losses
documentation: Used to define losses explicitly (as a Consumer capability)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CCS
documentation: Represents Carbon Capture and Storage

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CoolingDemand
documentation: Describes the cooling demand of e.g. a building

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyDemand
documentation: Allows to describe the total energy demand when differentiation between energy carriers is not possible or required, otherwise e.g. ElectricityDemand or HeatingDemand is an alternative

classes.name: esdl.esdl.MobilityDemand
documentation: Energy demand of the mobility sector. Allows to specify the vehicle types, fuel types and their efficiency and distance travelled

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GasStorage
documentation: Defines a gas storage asset, see also CCS

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EnergyNetwork
documentation: Defines an energy network. Used for national energy balances, when the specific energy carrier is not required

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractConductor
documentation: Abstract class to describe conductors such as cables and pipes and joining them using a joint

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractSwitch
documentation: Abstract class to describe switches such as valve and a circuit breaker

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractTransformer
documentation: Abstract class to describe transformers, such as Heat exchangers, transformers and pumps

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractConnection
documentation: Abstract class to describe connections of a building to a grid. E.g. a heat connection, gas connection and electricity connection

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PVTInstallation
documentation: Defines an installation that combines PV and thermal energy collection

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SinkConsumer
documentation: (Deprecated, will be removed in future ESDL versions) Represents a consumer that consumes exported energy from the current energy system. Used to model the rest of the energy system that is out of the current scope

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SourceProducer
documentation: (Deprecated, will be removed in future ESDL versions) Represents a source that delivers imported energy into the current energy system. Used to model the rest of the energy system that is out of the current scope

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ElectricityProducer
documentation: Describes a (generic) electricity producing asset

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HeatProducer
documentation: Describes a (generic) heat producing asset

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GasProducer
documentation: Describes a (generic) gas producing asset

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HybridHeatPump
documentation: Hybrid heatpump with both an electric heatpump and a gasheater part

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AbstractBasicConversion
documentation: Abstract class for all simple conversion assets that have a power and an efficiency attribute

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CAES
documentation: Compressed Air Energy Storage

classes.name: esdl.esdl.WindTurbine
documentation: Describes an individual wind turbine. A wind turbine is a producer capability

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PVPanel
documentation: Describes an individual PV panel. See PVInstallation for multiple PV panels. This is a Producer capability

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ElectricityNetwork
documentation: Describes an complete Electricty network, without detailing the complete topology. It is a Transport capability

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ElectricityCable
documentation: Describes a representation of an electricity cable. When defining the geometry of a cable by means of a line, the first point of the line refers to the first port and the last point of the line refers to the second port.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BuildingUnit
documentation: Describes a physical part of a building. In dutch 'verblijfsobject' in the BAG national building and address registry. This can be used e.g. to model appartments in appartment complexes

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GenericConversion
documentation: Generic conversion class that can be used in cases that the actual asset type is not important or not supported yet in ESDL

classes.name: esdl.esdl.AggregatedConversion
documentation: Represents an aggregation of multiple conversion assets as one aggregated conversion asset. It allows you to reference the conversion asset it is aggregated of by using the aggregationOf reference. Can be used to aggregate a heterogeneous collection of conversion assets (e.g. of different types)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GasHeater
documentation: Converts gas to heat, e.g. a gas boiler or gas heater

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HeatNetwork
documentation: Describes an complete heat network, without detailing the complete topology. It is a Transport capability

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GasNetwork
documentation: Describes an complete gas network, without detailing the complete topology. It is a Transport capability

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Pipe
documentation: Represents a pipe to transport gasses or fluids. When defining the geometry of a pipe by means of a line, the first point of the line refers to the first port and the last point of the line refers to the second port.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GeothermalSource
documentation: Geothermal source including the installation that connects the source to the network

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CoGeneration
documentation: Abstract asset describing a co-generation plant that produces heat and electricity

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HeatPump
documentation: Describes a Heat Pump

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Transformer
documentation: Electrical transformer between different voltage levels

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HeatExchange
documentation: Exchange heat between two circuits

classes.name: esdl.esdl.EConnection
documentation: Electricity connection of a building. Defines the demarcation between the inhouse network and the electricity grid (location where the (smart) meter is located)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.HConnection
documentation: Heat connection of a building. Defines the demarcation between the inhouse network and the heat grid (location where the (smart) meter is located)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GConnection
documentation: Gas connection of a building. Defines the demarcation between the inhouse network and the gas grid (location where the (smart) meter is located)

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PowerPlant
documentation: Defines an electricity generating plant

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PowerToX
documentation: Represents the ability to convert electricity to some other form of energy

classes.name: esdl.esdl.XToPower
documentation: Represents the ability to convert some other form of energy to electricity

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Pump
documentation: Defines a pump, e.g. in a water or heat network

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Airco
documentation: Represents an air conditioning unit

classes.name: esdl.esdl.SolarCollector
documentation: Defines a SolarCollector asset

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ResidualHeatSource
documentation: Defines a source of residual heat, e.g. a data center or factory

classes.name: esdl.esdl.FermentationPlant
documentation: Defines a plant fuelled by biomass

classes.name: esdl.esdl.GasConversion
documentation: Defines an asset that can convert gas into another form of gas. E.g. SMR or ATR.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.WaterToPower
documentation: Defines an asset that uses water to produce electricity. E.g.  hydro power, tidal power, wave power or osmotic power

classes.name: esdl.esdl.RoomHeater
documentation: Defines an asset for heating rooms, such as infra red panels, gas stove, etc.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.BiomassHeater
documentation: Converts biomass into heat and/or electricity

classes.name: esdl.esdl.UTES
documentation: Underground Thermal Energy Storage

classes.name: esdl.esdl.WaterBuffer
documentation: Storage by means of storing energy in water, e.g. a tank.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Joint
documentation: A Joint is a means to connect AbstractConductors, such as Pipes and ElectricalCables. This helps when these conductors have opposite Ports.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Building
documentation: Represents a physical building

classes.name: esdl.esdl.ATES
documentation: Aquifer Thermal Energy Storage

classes.name: esdl.esdl.FuelCell
documentation: Defines a Fuel Cell

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Valve
documentation: Defines a valve, e.g. in a water, gas or heat network

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CHP
documentation: Describes a Combined Heat and Power installation

classes.name: esdl.esdl.Electrolyzer
documentation: Defines an electrolyzer that converts electricity into hydrogen

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PVInstallation
documentation: Defines a Photo Voltaic Installation, e.g. roof top PV, a PV field or parc.

classes.name: esdl.esdl.CircuitBreaker
documentation: Defines a circuit breaker in electric transmission or distribution grids

classes.name: esdl.esdl.PVPark
documentation: Defines a PV park of multiple panels

classes.name: esdl.esdl.WindPark
documentation: Defines a wind park of multiple turbines
